s-1.ptxt: REPAIR INSTRUCTION

s2.ptxt: Clean the vehicle body surface.

s3.ptxt: Using a heat light, heat the vehicle body surface.

atten3.ptxt: Do not heat the vehicle body excessively.

s3.ptxt: Wipe off any tape adhesive residue with cleaner.

s2.ptxt: Installation temperature

s3.ptxt: When ambient temperature is below 15°C, perform the installation procedure after warming the vehicle body surface (installation surface of the door frame) and tape up to between 20 to 30°C using a heat light. When ambient temperature is above 35°C, cool the vehicle body surface (installation surface of the door frame) and tape down to between 20 to 30°C prior to installation.

item.ptxt: The most appropriate temperature for installing the tape is 25°C.
When the temperature is low, the tape turns stiff and comes off easily. When the temperature is high, the tape looses elasticity.

s2.ptxt: Before installation

s3.ptxt: Remove any roughness of coating and dirt on and around the vehicle body surface where the tape will be installed (installation surface of the door frame). If any roughness or dirt remains when pressing the tape onto the surface, air will be trapped under the tape and result in a poor appearance.

atten4.ptxt: Spray water on the shop floor to settle any dust.

s2.ptxt: Key points for handling the tape

s3.ptxt: The tape bends and rolls up easily. Store the tape between flat pieces of cardboard or other similar objects and keep it dry and level.

atten3.ptxt: Do not bend the tape or leave it in high temperature places.

s2.ptxt: Key points for installation of the tape (how to use a squeegee and installation procedure for flat surfaces)

item.ptxt: Conduct positioning of the tape with a high accuracy to achieve a neat finish and to avoid peeling.
The tape cannot be reused because it deforms or does not fit any more after being removed.

s3.ptxt: To avoid air bubbles, slightly raise the part of the tape that are going to be applied so that its adhesive surface does not touch the vehicle body while applying the tape. Tilt the squeegee at a 40 to 50° (for pressing forward) or 30 to 45° (for pulling) to the vehicle body surface and press the tape onto the vehicle body surface with a force of 20 to 30 N (2 to 3 kgf) at a constant slow speed of 3 to 7 cm per second.

atten3.ptxt: Be sure to observe the specified pressing speed, force, and angle of the squeegee to avoid wrinkles or air bubbles.

item.ptxt: Either angle of the squeegee (for pressing forward or for pulling) is acceptable.
Be sure to apply the tape while removing the release paper 10 to 20 mm from the edge of the squeegee.

s2.ptxt: Key points for installation of the tape (how to use a squeegee and installation procedure for hemming surfaces)

s3.ptxt: If it is difficult to press the tape, press it in several steps as shown in the illustration. Use your fingers or the padded surface of a squeegee to slowly apply the tape to the hem of the vehicle, especially for a small hem.

atten4.ptxt: When applying the tape to the backside of a hem, remove the release paper and use your fingers or the padded surface of a squeegee.

s2.ptxt: Key points for installation of the tape (how to use a squeegee and installation procedure for corners)

s3.ptxt: Remove the release paper and apply the tape carefully with your fingers.
Before applying the tape to each corner, heat the tape using a heat light and gradually apply it, to avoid wrinkles on the tape and achieve a neat finish.

s2.ptxt: Check after installation

s3.ptxt: After completing the application, check if the tape is applied neatly. If the tape is not applied neatly, reapply using new tape.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR LOWER OUTSIDE STRIPE

s2.ptxt: Refer to the illustration to position the rear door lower outside stripe.
Remove the release paper and apply the stripe.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR OUTSIDE STRIPE

atten3.ptxt: Be sure to install the rear door outside stripe after applying the rear door lower outside stripe.

s2.ptxt: Refer to the illustration to position the rear door outside stripe.
Remove the release paper and apply the stripe.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR UPPER WINDOW FRAME MOULDING

s2.ptxt: Install the rear door upper window frame moulding to the door frame.
Using an air riveter or hand riveter with a nose piece, install the rear door upper window frame moulding with 7 new rivets.

atten4.ptxt: If the rivet cannot be cut, pull it once and cut it.

item.ptxt: Do not pry the rivet with the riveter, as this will cause damage to the riveter and mandrel.
Confirm that the rivets are seated properly against the moulding. Do not tilt the riveter when installing the rivet to the moulding. Do not leave any space between the rivet head and moulding.
Do not leave any space between the moulding and door frame. Firmly hold together the 2 items while installing the rivet.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR FRONT WINDOW FRAME MOULDING

atten4.ptxt: When installing the rear door front window frame moulding, heat the vehicle body and rear door front window frame using a heat light.

atten3.ptxt: Do not heat the vehicle body and rear door window frame moulding excessively.

s2.ptxt: Clean the vehicle body surface.

s3.ptxt: Using a heat light, heat the vehicle body surface.
Remove the double-sided tape from the vehicle body.
Wipe off any tape adhesive residue with cleaner.

s2.ptxt: Clean the rear door front window frame moulding. (If reusing the rear door front window frame moulding)

s3.ptxt: Using a heat light, heat the rear door front window frame moulding.
Remove the double-sided tape from the rear door front window frame moulding.
Wipe off any tape adhesive residue with cleaner.
Apply new double-sided tape to the rear door front window frame moulding.

s2.ptxt: Install the rear door front window frame moulding.

s3.ptxt: Using a heat light, heat the vehicle body and rear door front window frame moulding.
Remove the release paper from the rear door front window frame moulding.

atten4.ptxt: After removing the release paper, keep the exposed adhesive free from foreign matter.

s3.ptxt: Engage the clip and install the rear door front window frame moulding.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR BELT MOULDING ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the clip and 6 claws, and install the rear door belt moulding assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR OUTSIDE HANDLE FRAME SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Apply MP grease to the sliding parts on the rear door outside handle frame sub-assembly.
Engage the claw to install the rear door outside handle frame sub-assembly.
Using a "TORX" socket wrench (T30), install the rear door outside handle frame sub-assembly with the screw.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR REAR OUTSIDE HANDLE PAD

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 claws to install the rear door rear outside handle pad.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR FRONT OUTSIDE HANDLE PAD

s2.ptxt: Engage the 3 claws to install the rear door front outside handle pad.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR OUTSIDE HANDLE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Insert the front end of the rear door outside handle assembly into the rear door outside handle frame.

atten3.ptxt: If the bellcrank lever is not pulled and held when installing the outside handle, the bellcrank lever will interfere with the outside handle and the release plate will be damaged.

s2.ptxt: Insert the rear end of the rear door outside handle assembly into the rear door outside handle frame. Next, slide the rear door outside handle assembly toward the front of the vehicle to install it.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR OUTSIDE HANDLE COVER

s2.ptxt: Engage the claws to install the rear door outside handle cover.
Using a "TORX" socket wrench (T30), install the rear door outside handle cover with the screw.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR INSIDE LOCKING CABLE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the rear door inside locking cable assembly.
Engage the 3 claws.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR LOCK REMOTE CONTROL CABLE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the rear door lock remote control cable assembly.
Engage the claw.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR LOCK ASSEMBLY

item.ptxt: When reusing the removed rear door lock assembly, replace the door lock wiring harness seal on the connector with a new one.
Do not allow grease or dust to adhere to the door lock wiring harness seal surface of the connector.

Reusing the door lock wiring harness seal or using a damaged door lock wiring harness seal may cause water intrusion to the connection. This may result in a malfunction of the rear door lock assembly.

s2.ptxt: Apply MP grease to the sliding parts of the rear door lock assembly.
Install a new door lock wiring harness seal to the rear door lock assembly.
Insert the rear door lock assembly to the rear door outside handle release plate, and set it to the rear door panel.
Make sure that the rear door outside handle frame release plate is securely connected to the rear door lock assembly.
Using a "TORX" socket wrench (T30), install the rear door lock assembly with the 3 screws.
Connect the connector.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY

atten3.ptxt: The regulator arm must be below the intermediate position when installing the power window regulator motor.

s2.ptxt: Using a "TORX" driver (T25), install the power window regulator motor assembly with the 3 screws.

atten4.ptxt: The new rear window regulator adopts a self-tapping method. The installation position of the regulator will be threaded when the self-tapping screw is inserted.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR WINDOW REGULATOR SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Apply MP grease to the sliding parts of the rear door window regulator sub-assembly.
Install the temporary bolt to the rear door window regulator sub-assembly.
Temporarily install the window regulator.
Tighten the 4 bolts in the order shown in the illustration to install the rear door window regulator.
Connect the connector.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR FRAME GARNISH

s2.ptxt: Engage the clip and install a new rear door frame garnish.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR GLASS RUN

s2.ptxt: Install the rear door glass run.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR GLASS SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Slide the rear door glass sub-assembly to install it as shown in the illustration.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR QUARTER WINDOW GLASS

s2.ptxt: Install the rear door quarter window glass to the rear door quarter window weatherstrip.
Install the rear door quarter window glass together with the rear door quarter window weatherstrip in the direction indicated by the arrow in the illustration.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR LOWER WINDOW FRAME SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Insert the rear door lower window frame sub-assembly from above.
Install the rear door glass run to the rear door lower window frame sub-assembly.
Install the rear door lower window frame sub-assembly with the 2 bolts and screw.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR WEATHERSTRIP

s2.ptxt: Engage the 20 clips and install the rear door weatherstrip.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR CHECK ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Install the rear door check assembly with the 3 bolts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR CHECK ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Install the rear door check assembly with the bolt and 2 nuts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR SERVICE HOLE COVER

s2.ptxt: Apply butyl tape to the rear door panel.
Pass the rear door lock remote control cable, rear door inside locking cable and each connector through a new rear door service hole cover.
Install the new rear door service hole cover.

atten3.ptxt: Securely install the rear door service hole cover avoiding wrinkles and air bubbles.

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector and clamp.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR INSIDE PANEL REINFORCE

s2.ptxt: Install 4 new grommets.
Install a new rear door inside panel reinforce with the 4 screws.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR INNER GLASS WEATHERSTRIP

s2.ptxt: Engage the 3 claws to install the rear door inner glass weatherstrip.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR ARMREST BASE PANEL UPPER

s2.ptxt: Engage the 4 claws.
Install the rear door armrest base panel upper with the 7 screws.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR POWER WINDOW REGULATOR SWITCH ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the 2 claws and install the rear power window regulator switch assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR INSIDE HANDLE SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Connect the rear door lock remote control cable and rear door inside locking cable to the rear inside handle.
Engage the 2 claws and install the rear door inside handle sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR TRIM BOARD SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the claw to install the No. 1 door scuff plate clamp.
Connect each connector.
Engage the rear door trim board with the 4 clips of the rear door inner glass weatherstrip.
Engage the 7 clips to install the rear door trim board to the rear door panel.
Install the 3 screws.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL DOOR ASSIST GRIP COVER

s2.ptxt: Engage the 6 claws and install the door assist grip cover.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DOOR INSIDE HANDLE BEZEL PLUG

s2.ptxt: Engage the 3 claws to install the rear door inside handle bezel plug.

s-1.ptxt: INITIALIZE POWER WINDOW CONTROL SYSTEM
